Meet Your Candidates

President

Sieimur Veliiev
President

Candidate Profile

Let's be honest about where we are. TBSA lost its legacy after COVID. Two years ago, it was revived but revival is not the same as rebuilding. Right now, TBSA has no stable structure, no recognizable brand, limited funding, and a disconnect from the very students it exists to serve. Most business students do not know what TBSA is or do not see a reason to care. That is the reality we need to name. And I know exactly how to change it.

As VP Corporate Relations, I co-organized our Speed Networking event and led our Women in Leadership panel, bringing senior executives to campus and creating real access for students. Before TBSA, I was selected among 20 pan-European representatives at the European Youth Parliament to develop post-war reconstruction strategies for European decision-makers. At the Red Cross, I raised over $20,000 in humanitarian support and guided international organizations through conflict zones alongside UNICEF, the BBC, and the New York Times. I co-organized Ukraine's largest job fair, attracting 2,500 students and 40 companies, with one third securing employment.

1. Rebuild the Foundation — I will rewrite our constitution and establish clear governance, defined roles, and real accountability. TBSA needs to function as an institution where structure outlasts any single year of leadership.

2. Earn Back Student Trust — We have a recognition problem. Business students do not know what TBSA offers because right now we do not offer enough. I will build a consistent event calendar around what students need: professional access, mentorship, career opportunities, and events worth attending.

3. Activate Our Umbrella Clubs — Our clubs are an underused asset. I will connect them through shared resources, coordinated initiatives, and a unified identity, turning isolation into a genuine ecosystem.

4. Formalize Key Institutional Relationships — TBSA needs to be at the table with the Student Union and School of Business, not knocking on the door. These partnerships unlock funding, credibility, and influence. I will build and maintain them.

5. Build a Brand Worth Recognizing — Students should know what TBSA stands for. I will establish a consistent, professional identity across every platform. Brand is not just internal — it is how we grow beyond Peterborough, build university partnerships, and open bigger doors for every member.

6. Create Financial Stability — I will pursue a proactive sponsorship strategy, build corporate partnerships, and fully utilize every institutional funding avenue. TBSA should operate from stability, not scarcity.

Whoever takes this role is not inheriting a running organization. They are accepting a mandate to build one. I am not promising a perfect year. I am promising an honest one, where we lay a foundation that holds. Business students at Trent deserve an association that fights for them and opens doors they could not open alone. That is what I am here to build. If you want TBSA rebuilt the right way — I am asking for your vote.

VP Finance

Jackson Greenfield-Paine
VP Finance

Candidate Profile

I appreciate the opportunity to apply for the VP of Finance position at the TBSA. Following my time as the Director of Finance, I have seen firsthand how the union operates and how the community relies on our financial transparency. I am currently pursuing my undergraduate degree in Economics & Accounting and looking back at my progression from high school to my roles here at Trent, it has prepared me in aspects of how I now operate as a person overall, including how I am able to continue both my academic and professional life as a leader.

While in my previous role as Director of Finance, I learned to adapt to the specific needs of the TBSA, taking the most meaningful action to ensure our budgets and reports were handled accurately. Just as I did during my time as Assistant Supervisor at Little Caesars, I made it a point to understand the daily operations of the office. I took pride in being someone who could step into the details when needed, using my efficiency and communication skills to help other members of the team understand our financial standing.

I underwent significant seminars and training. I learned how careers were formed out of what I had initially picked out of a passion for media creation, and having been on the Director side of the TBSA, I have thorough knowledge in a variety of areas which I still plan on maintaining. I plan on using this background to help provide a perspective on how we can improve our financial reporting, ensuring the TBSA remains organized and prepared for the future.

Going forward I want to make sure that my work as VP of Finance at the TBSA is as involved as possible, I plan to take leadership, ownership and continuance of the people who came before me. Looking at what this community has built, it is only right that we keep it moving along as planned, I understand the work that needs to go into this role, and I plan to do so with elegancy. I will do as I always and maintain a steady pace as I enter this new direction in my career.

VP Corporate Relations

Lauren Bressanutti
VP Corporate Relations

Candidate Profile

As a candidate for Vice President of Corporate Relations, my goal is to strengthen TBSA's presence within the business community while creating meaningful opportunities for students. Having served as Director of Corporate Relations, I have already gained hands-on experience building partnerships, communicating with sponsors, and representing the TBSA externally. I understand what works, where we can improve, and how to elevate our outreach strategy to the next level. I am ready to use that experience to expand our impact in a more strategic and sustainable way.

Even before the TBSA, I have worked in roles that have prepared me and supplied me with skills needed for this position. Through my experience working in the Marketing and Communications department for the York School Board as well as an administrative assistant with a Contracting Company, I have developed the confidence and professionalism required to reach out to local, regional, and national businesses effectively.

As VP, I will work towards refining our sponsorship strategy by creating structured outreach plans, sponsorship packages, and long-term partnership goals. My focus will be on building mutually beneficial relationships by ensuring sponsors receive value and visibility while students gain access to networking, mentorship as well as career opportunities.

Serving in Corporate Relations has shown me the importance of consistency and follow-up. I will prioritize maintaining strong communication with alumni and corporate partners through organized tracking systems, appreciation initiatives and ongoing engagement opportunities. Rather than one-time collaborations, I want the TBSA to cultivate relationships that will continue to grow long after I've graduated.

Building on the connections and outreach skills I've already developed, I will work to secure diverse and accomplished professionals across industries to speak at our events. My goal is to bring in speakers who will provide practical career advice, real-world insight and networking opportunities that benefit students long after the event ends.

Having already represented TBSA in an external capacity, I understand the importance of professionalism, responsiveness and reliability. As VP, I will continue to ensure that every interaction reflects positively on the TBSA and strengthens our reputation within the business community.

VP Human Resources

Kayla Hoare
VP Human Resources

Candidate Profile

A vote for Hoare is a vote for more: More connection, more support, and more room to grow.

Hi everyone! My name is Kayla Hoare, and I'm a first-year student in the Trent/Swansea B.B.A. and L.L.B. dual degree program. From the moment I got to Trent, I've been looking for ways to get involved, meet new people, and find my place within the business community. Running for VP Human Resources feels like a natural step, because at the end of the day, what makes any team great is the people behind it.

For me, HR is all about people. It's about creating a space where everyone feels comfortable, supported, and genuinely excited to be part of something bigger than themselves. In my current role as a Human Resources Student Office Assistant at Trent, I've learned how to manage stress, stay organized, and navigate difficult conversations in a calm and professional way. My previous co-op experience with Community Living is where I really discovered my passion for HR. It showed me how important empathy, fairness, and strong communication are in building a healthy team environment.

If elected, my main goal is simple: I want every TBSA member to feel like they belong. Not just in their role, but as a person. I'll work to keep the team environment positive and inclusive by encouraging open communication and making sure everyone feels heard and supported.

I also want to make onboarding smoother and more welcoming. Starting something new can be intimidating, so I want members to feel confident and excited from day one. Evaluations will be more than just check-ins, they'll be a chance to reflect, grow, and celebrate everything members are contributing.

When it comes to concerns or conflict, I'm someone who listens first. I will always approach situations with fairness, understanding, and confidentiality, making sure everyone feels respected and taken seriously.

Lastly, I'm really passionate about growth and development. Through my involvement in the CLC Leadership Competition, Kappa Sigma Psi, and various micro-credentials, I've seen how powerful it is to keep learning and trying new things. I'd love to bring more of those opportunities into TBSA.

At the end of the day, I believe that when people feel supported and connected, everything else falls into place.

So, a vote for Hoare is a vote for more... more community, more growth, and more of what makes TBSA amazing!

VP Technology

Od Duuren
VP Technology

Candidate Profile

As a second-year Business Information Systems student, my goal is to enhance TBSA's marketing. I will ensure that TBSA can reach and connect every student at Trent University to all opportunities, networks, and events.

I have hands-on technical expertise to prove that I'm fit for the role and the passion to contribute something meaningful, and I mean it. I'm great at visual storytelling and applying aesthetic pleasure to content as I'm an experienced and educated photographer who's had extensive experience in freelancing–events, volunteers, headshots, etc. I'm proficient in Adobe Premiere Pro for video editing, Photoshop, and Lightroom, ensuring that promotional videos and graphics will be on point.

If elected, I will focus on building a high-impact engagement with meaningful interactions and improving attendance. Committing to working closely with the team, using my soft skills. Finally digitally innovate TBSA.

Marketing is something I can contribute to with great enthusiasm. My own father used to be a great marketing manager, thus I believe I have what it takes with all the practical and soft skills I have in me to produce the necessary mindset to grow the brand.

VP Events

Krishna Thakkar
VP Events

Candidate Profile

My name is Krishna Thakkar, and I am seeking your vote for Vice President of Events for the 2026-2027 academic year. My goal is to elevate the TBSA event experience by applying high professional standards to every stage of planning and execution.

My strongest qualification for this role is my current position as a Student Ambassador for Lady Eaton College (LEC). Working within the Professional College Office, approximately 50% of my role is dedicated to the hosting and logistical support of college-wide events. This experience is directly transferable to the VP Events mandate: I act as a primary point of contact for college programming, working under professional staff to ensure events run on schedule and meet university standards. My work in the College Office has provided me with a clear understanding of the administrative hierarchy at Trent, including how the College Cabinet functions and the protocols for student organization support. I have fostered professional connections with staff and faculty, developing the communication skills necessary to represent the TBSA as a mature and organized student body.

If elected, I will use my experience with Trent's administrative structures to deliver a more efficient and professional event calendar. I will facilitate 'Mixers with Meaning' — events designed to connect business students with industry mentors and local leaders in a structured, professional environment. I will use my knowledge of collegiate protocols to ensure that when the TBSA reaches out to the College Cabinets or Offices for support, our proposals are of the highest professional quality, meeting all university requirements for funding and resource allocation. I will apply the logistical discipline I use in my Ambassador role to the TBSA — detailed event itineraries, proactive communication within the executive team, and a focus on day-of execution that ensures a seamless experience for all attendees.

I am a candidate who understands the standards of Trent University. I have the experience to represent the TBSA professionally and the skills to execute events that provide real value to the business student community. Vote Krishna Thakkar for VP Events – Professionalism, Experience, and Execution.

No candidates found.
